$comment: D-CSUBSET-32BIT-ALU-FORMS (c73, 2026-07-01): a bit-field with a SUB-INT allocation UNIT (u8/u16/signed char) must compute its extract/insert RMW arithmetic at the PROMOTED int width, else the synthesized shift/mask/and/or ops wall at the LIR sub-native ALU gate ('integer TypeKind ordinal 6 [U8] / 7 [U16] has no native-width ALU forms'). c71+c72 promoted the char/sub-int ALU at the source-operator sites; the post-c72 sqlite3.c re-probe left 2 errors on U8 bit-fields — the bit-field read/write is SYNTHESIZED at emitBitfieldExtract/emitBitfieldInsert (hir_to_mir) directly on the u8 storage unit, bypassing combineBinary. Probe confirmed: `struct{u8 a:3;}` read/write/++ all error; `unsigned a:3` (int unit) OK. FIX: both functions promote a sub-int unit (unitBytes*8 < 32) to i32 for the shift/mask/and/or, then Trunc the result/merged-unit back to the field type for the caller-node contract / Store; a native (>=32-bit) unit keeps computeTy==fieldTy = the original path (byte-identical, incl. wide u64:40). The aggregate-init caller coerces the init value to the field type first. This corpus exercises read + write + RMW neighbour-preservation + 3-bit overflow truncation (9&7=1) + signed-char sign-extension (-3) + aggregate init + a u16 unit: f.a+f.b=25 +3 +1 +4 +5 +4 => 42. Observables are signedness-agnostic (unsigned fields, positive) or explicit `signed char`, so it matches gcc on x86 (signed char) + aarch64 (UNSIGNED char), O0/O2. RED-ON-DISABLE: revert the promote -> error[L_UnsupportedLoweringForOpcode] '<deferred>: integer TypeKind ordinal 6/7 ...' (compile failure, not 42). gcc/clang compile it and exit 42. Sibling of char_alu_integer_promotion (c71) / unary_bitnot_neg_promotion (c72), same anchor family.
